channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump expected to pick Howard Lutnick for Commerce secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to nominate Howard Lutnick to serve as Commerce secretary, a source familiar told The Hill. Lutnick is the chair and CEO of Cantor Fitzgerald and is also currently serving as co-chair of the Trump transition.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps Sean Duffy, Fox host and former congressman, for transportation secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has picked Sean Duffy, a Fox Business host and former Republican congressman from Wisconsin, to head the Department of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Two women testified before the House Ethics Committee that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid them to have sex, according to the attorney representing them.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump dance takes over sports world
item.description: Supporters of President-elect Donald Trump have been paying homage on the gridiron and inside the octagon in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Biden asks Congress for about $100 billion in disaster relief funding
item.description: The largest chunk of funding — about $40 billion — would be for FEMA's disaster relief fund, so that it has enough money to last through the coming year. But there are requests for 16 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: House Ethics Committee to meet Wednesday amid debate over Gaetz report
item.description: Members of the House Ethics Committee will meet Wednesday, a source confirmed to The Hill, as the panel weighs whether it should release its report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), whom President-elect Trump plans to nominate to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal workers brace for Trump overhaul of civil service
item.description: President-elect Trump’s plans to reshape the federal workforce would allow him to fire wide swaths of career employees and replace them with political appointees — a move that comes as he has demanded loyalty at every level of government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps FCC commissioner Brendan Carr to chair the agency
item.description: Trump said he first nominated Carr to the FCC in 2017 and he was confirmed unanimously by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk is hiring for DOGE. Here's the type of people he wants to recruit
item.description: "This will send shockwaves through the system, and anyone involved in government waste, which is a lot of people," Musk said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Ron Johnson says his 'bias would be to vote' for Trump's picks
item.description: Sen. Ron Johnson, R-Wis., said he doesn't "particularly like being the human resource department of administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Big voter turnout this year benefited Republicans, contradicting conventional political wisdom
item.description: The 2024 presidential election featured sky-high turnout, approaching the historic levels of the 2020 contest and contradicting long-held conventional political wisdom that Republicans struggle to win races in which many people vote.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida congressional leaders demand answers from FEMA political scandal
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - Republican and Democratic leaders on Capitol Hill are preparing for a hearing next week with FEMA administrator Deanne Criswell, regarding alleged political discrimination after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden hosts Trump at White House in post-election tradition
item.description: President-elect Trump and President Biden are readying for what promises to be a highly awkward meeting at the White House on Wednesday as Biden welcomes his successor — a man he labeled a danger to the country — to his residence.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9
